ground_truth: data/realExam2026/reading/academicPassage.json (passage text reliable; questions UNDER-extracted ~3.2/passage) + hand-coded question clusters from .codex-tmp/ocr/*阅读*.txt (true structure)
notes: CRITICAL CONTEXT: the 2026改后 reading section is module-based (Module 1 = 35 Qs, Module 2 = 15 Qs) and INTERLEAVES three item types on one screen flow: Complete-the-Words (CTW, Q1-20), Read-In-Daily-Life (RIDL signs/notices/receipts/reviews, one Q each), and Academic Passage (AP). AP items appear as CLUSTERS of exactly 5 questions shown one-per-screen with the full passage on the left. The structured JSON under-extracted AP questions (mean 3.2/passage) and contains ~3 RIDL-leakage items; question-type distribution is therefore HAND-CODED from OCR (n=70 over 14 fully-read clusters). The passage-LENGTH numbers in lib/readingGen (mean 317.5, target 280-360) and the opening-strategy claim (received_wisdom 46%) are inherited from OLD classic-TOEFL samples and are WRONG for 2026改后 — real 2026 AP passages are ~180-200 words and open with a direct topic/definition statement.

dimensions.name: questions_per_passage
dimensions.detector: Count single-question screens sharing one passage title within a module block (OCR). JSON count is unreliable (DeepSeek under-extracted).
examples: Value Theory: Q26-30 (5)
Synthetic Diamonds: Q31-35 (5)
Space Debris: Q11-15 (5, Module 2)
dimensions.gap: none (count matches); the gap is in the TYPE mix, not the count

dimensions.name: passage_word_count
dimensions.detector: whitespace word count of passage; RIDL-leakage excluded by header/receipt/review regex
examples: Thinking Outside the Box = 200w
Value Theory = 190w
Augmented Reality for Training = 175-203w
dimensions.gap: BIG GAP. Gen is ~15% longer on average and has a long tail to 322w that does NOT exist in real (real hard ceiling ~210w). 42/156 gen passages exceed the entire real range. apPromptBuilder targets 280-360 (acceptable 200-440) — this is the OLD classic-TOEFL number and is ~70-90% too long for 2026改后.

dimensions.name: paragraph_count
dimensions.detector: OCR paragraph breaks (JSON lost them — JSON paragraphs field is mostly 1 and unreliable). Hand-read from 14 clusters.
examples: Value Theory = 3 paragraphs
Approximation Theory = 4 paragraphs
Urban Resilience = 3 paragraphs
dimensions.gap: small/none — gen 3-4 paras matches real 2-4 (mode 3). The structured JSON cannot measure this (lost paragraph breaks); confirmed via OCR.

dimensions.name: avg_sentence_length
dimensions.detector: words / sentence-count per passage (sentence split on .!?)
dimensions.gap: moderate — gen sentences run ~1.7 words longer on average with a heavier long tail (max 29 vs real 22). Tracks the over-length passage problem.

dimensions.name: question_type_distribution
dimensions.detector: classify stem by regex (closest-in-meaning=vocab, four-locations=insert_text, refer-to=reference, EXCEPT/NOT=negative_factual, why-mention/purpose-of-para=rhetorical_purpose, relate-to/how-does-para=paragraph_relationship, can-be-inferred/suggests=inference, main-point=main_idea, identify-the-sentence=sentence_select, according-to/what-is=factual_detail). Hand-validated against 14 read clusters.
examples: vocab: 'The word "mitigate" in the passage is closest in meaning to'
insert_text: 'There are four locations [■] in the passage that indicate where the following sentence could be added... Where would the sentence best fit?'
reference: 'What does "its cautionary implications" refer to in the passage?'
negative_factual: 'Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a threat to coral reefs in the passage?'
dimensions.current: gen has 0% insert_text, 0% reference, 0% sentence_select; over-produces paragraph_relationship (13.6% vs 2.9%) and vocabulary (20% vs 17%).
dimensions.gap: BIGGEST GAP. (1) insert_text (real 11.4%, gen 0%) is entirely missing — it is the single most consistent slot in a real cluster. (2) reference (real 4.3%, gen 0%) and sentence_select (real 1.4%, gen 0%) missing. (3) paragraph_relationship is 4.7x over-produced. The current QUESTION_PLANS in apPromptBuilder.js have no insert_text/reference/sentence_select and use paragraph_relationship in 4 of 5 plans.

dimensions.name: insert_text_present_and_last
dimensions.detector: stem matches /four locations|best fit|could be added/; check its index within the 5-question cluster
examples: Synthetic Diamonds Q35 (last): 'And because of the controlled conditions in which they are produced, synthetic diamonds are actually preferred for industrial uses... Where would the sentence best fit?'
Smart Textiles Q30 (last): 'Imagine being able to change the appearance of your furnishings depending on your mood or the season.'
Urban Resilience Q35 (last): 'For example, Singapore's Intelligent Transport System incorporates artificial intelligence (AI) to predict real-time traffic conditions...'
dimensions.current: 0 insert_text questions in the entire generated bank; passages carry no [■] insertion markers
dimensions.gap: BIG GAP. ~57% of real AP clusters end with an insert_text Q (always the LAST question, requiring 4 [■] markers placed at paragraph/sentence boundaries in the passage). Generator produces neither the markers nor the question type. Requires passage to expose 4 insertion slots.

dimensions.name: vocabulary_per_passage
dimensions.detector: count stems matching /closest in meaning/ per cluster
examples: 'The word "replicating" in the passage is closest in meaning to' (recreating / analyzing / altering / enhancing)
'The word "harness" in the passage is closest in meaning to' (exploit / limit / distribute / release)
'The phrase "in turn" in the passage is closest in meaning to' (as a later consequence / in all cases / unfortunately / before that)
dimensions.current: 20% of all gen questions are vocab (= ~1 per 5-question passage), so count roughly matches
dimensions.gap: count roughly matches (~1/passage), but see vocab_option_length — gen vocab options are too long/wordy vs real (real vocab options are mostly single words).

dimensions.name: options_per_question
dimensions.detector: len(options); JSON stores options as array of 4
dimensions.gap: none

dimensions.name: option_word_length_overall
dimensions.detector: whitespace word count per option; split vocab (single-word) vs non-vocab
examples: non-vocab option: 'producing synthetic diamonds is less harmful to the environment than mining natural diamonds is' (14w, a real long boundary case)
dimensions.gap: none on means — gen matches real closely (non-vocab ~7.8-8.0).

dimensions.name: option_length_spread_within_question
dimensions.detector: per-question (max option words - min option words); rate where spread <= 3
dimensions.gap: moderate (reverse direction). Gen options are TOO uniform (spread 1.6, 94% within 3w) vs real (spread 2.6, only 69% within 3w). The OPTION LENGTH RULE in apPromptBuilder ('within 2 words of each other') over-corrected; real ETS tolerates a 3-4 word spread. Over-uniformity is itself a synthetic tell.

dimensions.name: vocab_option_length
dimensions.detector: word count of options on stems matching /closest in meaning/
examples: real vocab options: distributing / acquiring / increasing / tracking (1 word each)
real vocab options: strength / threat / knowledge / supervision (1 word each)
dimensions.gap: moderate. Real vocab answer choices are overwhelmingly SINGLE words (mean 1.5); gen vocab options run ~2.9 words (phrases). Generator should output single-word synonyms for most vocab items.

dimensions.name: correct_is_uniquely_longest
dimensions.detector: per-question: is correct option the unique max-length option
dimensions.gap: none — gen 18.1% is below the 25% ceiling. (Real not directly measurable: structured JSON has no answer key, so target is the ETS_FLAVOR guideline of <=25%.) Solved dimension.

dimensions.name: answer_position_balance
dimensions.detector: tally correct_answer letter across all questions
dimensions.gap: none — gen is near-uniform. (Real answer key absent from structured JSON; cannot compute real position bias — deferred. Gen's pre-assigned balanced positions in apPromptBuilder work.)

dimensions.name: passage_opening_strategy
dimensions.detector: classify first sentence: definition markers (refers to / is defined as / investigates / is a / has emerged as) vs received-wisdom openers (Traditionally / Historically / While early...)
examples: 'Value theory investigates the nature of values and the principles that determine what is worth pursuing...'
'Urban green spaces, such as parks, gardens, and green roofs, provide numerous benefits to cities.'
'Parallel algorithms, a computing method common in modern computers, solve problems faster by dividing a task into smaller parts...'
dimensions.current: apPromptBuilder/ETS_FLAVOR prescribe openingStrategies received_wisdom_then_revision=0.46 ('Historically...', 'While early theories...')
dimensions.gap: BIG GAP (wrong target inherited). Real 2026 AP almost never opens with 'received wisdom then revision' (1/42). It opens with a DIRECT topic/definition statement, frequently naming+defining the subject in sentence 1. The 46% received-wisdom target should be cut to ~5% and direct-definition raised to ~90%.

dimensions.name: first_sentence_defines_subject
dimensions.detector: count definition patterns per passage (known as / called / refers to / defined as / , a X / , which); confirm at least one in paragraph 1
examples: 'Floating wind turbines are a special type of wind turbines because they are not fixed to the seabed but are anchored using advanced mooring systems...' + 'anchoring refers to securing the turbine in place...'
'coral bleaching, a phenomenon where corals lose their color and essential symbiotic algae'
'algorithms-step-by-step procedures that solve problems efficiently'
dimensions.gap: gen over-produces definition markers (2.1/passage vs real 1.23). Real AP introduces ~1 defined term (often the topic itself); gen stuffs ~2. Mild over-cooking.

dimensions.name: hedging_density
dimensions.detector: count /may|might|could|appear|seem|suggest|tend|often|generally|likely|perhaps|possibly|potentially/ per passage
examples: real: 'this could be attributed not to generational identity but to life-stage effects'
real: 'some assumptions merit scrutiny'
dimensions.gap: moderate-large. Gen hedges ~3x more than real (2.94 vs 1.03 per passage). Real AP is more declarative; hedging concentrates in the final 'limitation' move, not throughout. The prompt rule 'include 1-2 hedging words' is fine but generation over-applies it (and over a shorter passage, density is even higher).

dimensions.name: contrast_transition_density
dimensions.detector: count /however|although|though|while|yet|despite|nevertheless|nonetheless|whereas|conversely/ per passage
examples: real: 'However, this theory faces objections.'
real: 'Despite these efforts, challenges remain.'
dimensions.gap: moderate. Gen uses ~2x the contrast transitions of real (2.29 vs 1.09). Real AP typically has ONE strong pivot ('However,' / 'Despite') near the limitation move; gen sprinkles them.

dimensions.name: passive_voice_density
dimensions.detector: count /(is|are|was|were|be|been|being) + V-ed|V-en/ per sentence
dimensions.gap: small-moderate. Gen ~2x more passive (0.19 vs 0.10 per sentence). Prompt mandates 'passive voice in at least 1 sentence' which is fine; gen overshoots. (Note: ETS_FLAVOR.passivePerSentence=0.23 is the OLD classic-TOEFL number; real 2026 is 0.10.)

dimensions.name: lexical_register
dimensions.detector: char length per word; share of words >=7 chars; content-word / total
dimensions.gap: small. Gen is slightly heavier lexically (long-word ratio 0.404 vs 0.371; word length 5.88 vs 5.63). Lexical density matches (0.704 vs 0.709). Gen leans a touch more 'academic/dense' than real.

dimensions.name: topic_diversity
dimensions.detector: count distinct topic labels; real uses free-text topic (near 1:1 with passages); gen uses a 14-category enum
examples: real topics: Value Theory, Plant Communication, Augmented Reality for Training, Radio Astronomy, The Flynn Effect, Sociocybernetics, Floating Wind Turbines, Parallel Algorithms, Quantum Dots, Cybernetic Prosthetics, Generational Identity, Twin Stars

NOTE the real bias toward APPLIED tech / contemporary science (AR, cybernetics, quantum dots, parallel algorithms, smart textiles, urban resilience) — more 'modern applied' than the classic-TOEFL natural-history feel
dimensions.current: top 5 categories (biology/env/history/psych/geology) = 114/156 = 73% of bank; applied-tech topics under-represented
dimensions.gap: moderate. Real 2026 AP skews toward CONTEMPORARY APPLIED topics (computing, materials, biotech, urban systems, social science) with near-unique topics each time. Gen over-concentrates on biology/environmental/history/psychology/geology (classic-TOEFL natural-history canon). TOPIC_POOL exists but the 5-topic concentration persists.

correlations: DIFFICULTY is driven by ABSTRACTION + density, not length. The hardest real passages (Value Theory, Space Debris, Generational Identity) are NOT longer (all ~180-200w) — they are denser/more abstract (philosophy, orbital mechanics, social theory) and pair with inference/reference/EXCEPT questions and longer option text. Easy passages (Plant Communication, Coral Reef) are concrete process descriptions with factual_detail questions. So: control difficulty via topic abstraction and question-type mix, NOT by lengthening the passage.

The insert_text question (Q5) CO-REQUIRES passage scaffolding: the passage must expose 4 candidate insertion points [■] at paragraph/clause boundaries, and the inserted sentence is always a concrete elaboration/example that extends an existing point ('And because...', 'For example, Singapore's...', 'Imagine being able to...'). Generating the question without redesigning the passage to carry markers is impossible — these two dimensions must be built together.

A real 5-cluster has a near-fixed COMPOSITION recipe, not a free type draw: ~1 vocabulary (single-word options) + ~1 insert_text (last, ~57% of clusters) + a mix of factual_detail / inference / rhetorical_purpose / negative_factual + occasionally one reference or paragraph_relationship. Vocabulary and insert_text are the two 'anchor' slots; the middle 3 questions vary. Over a 5-set, expect 3-4 distinct types (matches the existing minQuestionTypeDiversity=3 gate).

Option-length UNIFORMITY anti-correlates with authenticity: real options vary more (spread 2.6) than gen's over-polished 1.6. Combined with gen's slightly heavier lexis and 2-3x hedging/contrast/passive, the cumulative effect is that gen passages read 'too smooth / too academic-template' — the synthetic tell is over-regularity, not any single metric.

deferred.reason: structured JSON has no answer key and OCR option-order is unreliable; cannot compute real A/B/C/D correct-answer distribution. Keep gen's near-uniform balance as the target.
no real answer key, so the <=25% target is the inherited ETS_FLAVOR guideline, not a measured 2026 number. Gen at 18.1% is presumed acceptable.

requires the correct answer + per-distractor semantic labeling against the passage; not machine-extractable from current data and not hand-labeled at scale. apPromptBuilder's per-type distractor recipes look directionally right (e.g. inference->opposite/not_mentioned) but the SHARE targets in AP_PROFILE.distractorPatternTargets are unverified for 2026.

classic TOEFL has 'which sentence best expresses essential info' (highlighted-sentence simplification); NOT observed in any of the 14 hand-read 2026 clusters. Either absent or very rare in 2026改后 AP — do not generate it as a staple. (Note: the related 'identify the sentence that...' SELECT-IN-PASSAGE type WAS seen once.)

hand-coded 8/14 clusters had insert_text=Q5, but the 6 without may reflect OCR truncation of the last screen rather than true absence. Real rate is plausibly higher than 57% (could be ~1/passage). Target set conservatively; revisit with more clean clusters.
